questions.question: What is the default metaclass used to create classes in Python?
questions.explanation: The built-in 'type' is the default metaclass in Python. When you write a class statement, Python calls type() to create the class object. type is itself a class whose instances are other classes.

questions.question: To specify a custom metaclass for a class, you use the ___ keyword argument in the class header.
questions.explanation: In Python 3, you specify a metaclass with the syntax 'class MyClass(metaclass=MyMeta): ...'. This tells Python to use MyMeta instead of the default type to create the class object.

questions.question: In a metaclass, what is the role of the __new__ method?
choices: It initializes instance attributes of the class
It creates and returns the new class object before it is initialized
It is called when an instance of the class is deleted
It defines the string representation of the class
questions.explanation: In a metaclass, __new__ is called first to create and return the new class object. It receives the metaclass, class name, bases tuple, and class dictionary. __init__ is called afterward to initialize the already-created class object.

questions.question: Which of the following correctly describes the class creation process when a metaclass is used?
choices: The class body runs, then metaclass.__init__ is called, then metaclass.__new__
metaclass.__new__ is called, then the class body runs, then metaclass.__init__
The class body runs to produce a namespace dict, then metaclass.__new__ creates the class, then metaclass.__init__ initializes it
metaclass.__init__ creates the class, then the class body runs
questions.explanation: Python first executes the class body to build a namespace dictionary of attributes. Then it calls the metaclass's __new__ method to create the class object, followed by __init__ to perform any additional initialization.

questions.question: The three-argument form of type() called as type(name, bases, dict) dynamically creates and returns a new ___ object.
questions.explanation: Calling type(name, bases, dict) with three arguments is the dynamic equivalent of a class statement. It creates and returns a new class object with the given name, base classes, and attribute dictionary.
